text: Artificial intelligence is advancing rapidly, and many experts predict that within a few decades we will see the emergence of Artificial General Intelligence, or AGI. Unlike current AI, which can only perform specific tasks like playing chess or translating languages, AGI would be able to think, learn, and reason across almost any topic, much like a human. Potential benefits include breakthroughs in healthcare, education, and solving climate change. However, risks such as job displacement, ethical concerns, and loss of control must be carefully managed. While no one knows the exact timeline, many researchers believe AGI could arrive between 2050 and 2100, depending on progress and investment.
